title: Peppery Dutch Duck Stir-fry
description: A dense and peppery dutch dish featuring duck legs with a paleo-friendly twist.
instructions: Season duck legs with salt and black pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring frequently to prevent burning.
Place the duck legs in the skillet skin-side down. Pour enough water into the pan (about 2 cups) to cover half of the duck legs.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 30 minutes or until a meat thermometer inserted near the bone registers 165°F (74°C).
Remove from heat and garnish with fresh thyme leaves and paprika. Serve hot as a main dish.
